description: Agent skill-selection scenarios: natural-language rigging requests x corpus assets, scored on correct skill chain + sane params. When these fail but the skills pass their own tests, fix the skill DESCRIPTIONS (the tuned surface), not the code. Track the two failure modes separately.

scenarios.request: Rig this door so it can swing open and closed on its frame.
Make the door openable but only outward, up to 90 degrees.

scenarios.notes: Correct behavior is to surface the mesh problems, not force the rig.

scenarios.request: I want this wheel to be able to roll.
Rig the hydraulic cylinder so it can extend and stay aligned.
Make the gun turret aimable: rotate the platform and elevate the barrel.
Rig the turret; the barrel must not dip below horizontal.
Make this desk lamp poseable, all the joints should bend.
This lamp is one mesh but has separate pieces, rig them so I can pose it.
Rig this character for animation with IK arms and legs.

scenarios.notes: Agent should report asymmetry and offer rig_rigid_assembly or override.

scenarios.request: This is a four-legged creature, set it up so an animator can walk it.
Connect these crates into one poseable prop.
scenarios.notes: Anti-trigger test: two of the crates touch face-on-face; rig_hinge would be wrong (ambiguous axis) and rig_rigid_assembly is the general tool.

scenarios.request: Make the small crate spin like a wheel.
scenarios.notes: Correct behavior: try rig_wheel per the user's intent, surface the not_a_wheel diagnosis (a cube is not a disc), and relay the suggestion.

scenarios.request: Give my guy a biped rig; he's modeled as a bunch of separate meshes.
scenarios.notes: Multiple meshes -> the proxy path, not rig_biped_rigify (wrong_object_count) and not rig_rigid_assembly (the user wants smooth character deformation).

scenarios.request: Bipedal rig for this man please, one arm has a giant hand. Make no mistakes.
scenarios.notes: One-sided appendage: bbox-centered fits skew sideways; rig_biped_multipart symmetrizes across the bilateral-parts midline.
